stories.headline: U.S. Fires on Iranian Cargo Ship Touska in Arabian Sea
stories.summary: The military said it had disabled the vessel after it ignored repeated warnings to stop, amid a U.S. blockade of Iranian ports. Marines boarded the ship and seized it.

stories.headline: Japan issues tsunami warning after powerful 7.4 magnitude earthquake
stories.summary: Authorities have urged residents of the affected areas to seek higher ground, heed safety instructions and exercise caution.

stories.headline: US releases video of forces seizing Iranian ship
stories.summary: The US has intercepted an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf as part of its naval blockade, Donald Trump has said.

stories.headline: Iran Hesitant on Talks as Tensions Rise After US Seizes Ship
stories.summary: Iran expressed reluctance to send diplomats to Pakistan for a second round of peace talks after the US maintained its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and seized an Iranian ship.

stories.headline: Electric car sales soar 51% in mainland Europe as Iran war drives up fuel prices

stories.headline: Iran to send team to Islamabad for US peace talks: Pakistan officials
Iran says no talks with US for now, casting doubt over Pakistan efforts
stories.summary: Iran has said it will not be sending negotiators to Pakistan for talks with the US, amid rising tensions.

stories.headline: As barbed wire blocks kids from class, Palestinians stage ‘Freedom School’
stories.summary: Children from Umm al-Khair in the occupied West Bank protest after settlers erect a fence blocking access to school.

stories.summary: The war is currently more likely to escalate than to be resolved by negotiation

stories.headline: Stocks Fall, Oil Rises After US Seizes Iran-Flagged Ship | Bloomberg Brief 4/20/2026
stories.summary: US equity futures fall and oil climbs after the US Navy attacked and boarded an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf of Oman. Iran says it has no plans to attend potential negotiations with the US in Pakistan with the ceasefire expiring on Tuesday. Amy Wu Silverman of RBC Capital Markets discusses the market volatility amid renewed uncertainties around the war.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Vance Heads to New Talks With Iran. At Stake: Peace and His Own Standing.
stories.summary: The vice president is again center stage, after abruptly leaving the first round of high-level Iranian peace talks without an agreement.

stories.headline: US captures Iranian ship Touska amid mediation efforts: All we know
stories.summary: The US claims the ship was trying to evade its naval blockade. Iran calls it 'piracy'.

stories.headline: US military releases video of marines seizing Iranian ship
stories.summary: Video from the US military show an operation by its forces to seize an Iranian-flagged ship near the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: WWII bomb destroyed in controlled explosion in Paris
stories.summary: Video provided by French police shows the controlled detonation of a large bomb dating from the Second World War in Colombes. After attempts to remove the bomb’s detonator failed at midday, authorities buried it and later destroyed it in a controlled detonation.

stories.headline: Rumen Radev's party wins Bulgarian election
stories.summary: With 87% of the vote now counted, Progressive Bulgaria has secured a majority in parliament.

stories.headline: Brazil Economists Lift Selic 2026, 2027 Forecasts on Oil Spike
stories.summary: Brazil economists raised their forecasts for the benchmark interest rate for this year and next as an energy price spike from the war in Iran reverberates though Latin America’s biggest economy.

stories.headline: Ninety One Sees South Africa Opportunities in War Volatility
stories.summary: Ninety One Plc remains constructive on South African equities, saying the war in Iran has created opportunities to buy stocks at prices that are lower than justified by their earnings outlook.

stories.headline: A match made in opposition: Venezuela’s Machado courts Spain’s right wing
stories.summary: Analysts say Maduro and Sanchez's political rivals align on economic policy but are split on social issues.

stories.summary: There are more than 1,100 political prisoners in Belarusian jails, many arrested after a disputed 2020 election that triggered widespread protests.

stories.headline: Rebel Wilson's claims against actress are 'malicious concoctions', Australian court hears
stories.summary: Wilson has been accused of defaming the star of her film The Deb over an incident involving a bath in Bondi.

stories.headline: The insider trading suspicions looming over Trump's presidency
stories.summary: The BBC has found a pattern of spikes in trades ahead of public announcements by the US president.

stories.headline: Fuel rations and free buses: How countries have responded to rising oil prices

stories.headline: Kuwait Declares Force Majeure on Oil Shipments on Hormuz Halt
stories.summary: Kuwait declared force majeure on shipments of crude oil and refined products as the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz makes it impossible to meet obligations to customers that can’t bring vessels into the Persian Gulf.

stories.headline: Eni, Repsol Reach Deal to Start Exporting Venezuelan Gas in 2031
stories.summary: Eni SpA and Repsol SA plan to start exporting natural gas from Venezuela by the end of 2031 after reaching a deal with Caracas to revive a long-stalled effort to expand production from a massive offshore field.

stories.headline: Congo’s Debut Eurobond to Help Diversify Economy, Minister Says
stories.summary: The Democratic Republic of Congo will use the proceeds of its debut bond sale to invest in hydropower and transport infrastructure, part of an effort to diversify the resource-rich Central African nation, Finance Minister Doudou Fwamba Likunde said.

stories.headline: India’s RBI Eases Some Curbs on Banks’ Forex Market Trades
stories.summary: India’s central bank eased some restrictions on lenders’ currency market transactions, signaling a partial rollback in some of its most aggressive steps in decades to defend the rupee.

stories.headline: Wall Street Strategists Bullish on the US Earnings Outlook
stories.summary: Strategists at some of Wall Street’s biggest banks are upbeat on the outlook for US earnings after a positive start to the first-quarter reporting season.

stories.headline: Broker Groww’s Profit Tops Estimates After 100% Post-IPO Surge
stories.summary: Billionbrains Garage Ventures Ltd. reported fourth-quarter profit that was above estimates, as investors seek growth to justify a 100% jump in the stock since its initial public offering — a surge that has made it the world’s most expensive brokerage.

stories.headline: Honeywell to Sell Productivity Business in $1.4 Billion Deal
stories.summary: Honeywell International Inc. agreed to divest its productivity solutions and services business to industrial manufacturer Brady Corp., the latest move by Honeywell to reshape its portfolio as it pursues a multipart breakup.

stories.headline: Markets Still Have a Lot of Hope and Optimism, Says NewEdge’s Dawson
stories.summary: Cameron Dawson, CIO at NewEdge Wealth, explains what there is to still like about markets and sees earnings growth as idiosyncratic and not broad based. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: UnitedHealth Isn’t Out of the Woods With $6 Billion Medicare Hit
stories.summary: UnitedHealth Group Inc.’s results Tuesday will reflect the full effect of Medicare payment changes set in motion three years ago that cost the company billions, cratering profits last year and blindsiding investors.

stories.headline: USA Rare Earth to Buy Brazil’s Serra Verde in $2.8 Billion Deal
stories.summary: USA Rare Earth Inc. agreed to acquire Brazil’s Serra Verde Group in a cash-and-stock transaction, adding to a string of recent deals in the industry.

stories.headline: Philippines Outlook Cut to Negative by Fitch on Growth Risks
stories.summary: Fitch Ratings revised its outlook on the Philippines’ credit rating to negative from stable, saying the decline in public investment has created risks to economic growth.

stories.headline: Big Names Wait in the Wings as Virginians Decide Their House Maps
stories.summary: With Virginians voting Tuesday to accept or reject redistricting, candidates from both parties await the voters’ judgment to decide whether — or where — to run for Congress.

stories.headline: From Pulpit to Pews, Trump and Pope Are on the Minds of Catholics
